justification: All pass criteria met: off-by-one correctly identified (1-indexed API with 0-indexed formula producing page*pageSize instead of (page-1)*pageSize), correct fix stated, severity is Critical, safe_to_deploy is false, and input validation gaps (page<1, pageSize bounds) noted as secondary findings.

observations.text: Off-by-one root cause precisely explained with concrete reproduction: page=1 yields offset=10, skipping first 10 rows
Severity correctly rated Critical with clear business impact articulated
safe_to_deploy false with thoughtful rollback and backward-compatibility analysis
Input validation gap elevated to a separate critical finding covering negative offset, zero pageSize, and unbounded pageSize DoS
Additional valuable findings: SQL injection defense-in-depth, OFFSET scalability, SELECT * waste
SQL injection and DoS findings rated High/Critical may slightly over-scope for a pagination review, though they are legitimate defense-in-depth observations

agent_output_excerpt: {"summary":{"verdict":"BLOCK","one_liner":"1-indexed pagination contract is violated — page 1 silently skips the first pageSize rows, returning wrong data to every caller."},"critical_findings":[{"title":"Off-by-one error: page 1 skips first N results","risk":"Every API consumer requesting page=1 (the documented first page) gets page 2's data. The first pageSize orders are never visible to any user. This is silent data loss — no error, just wrong results.","severity":"Critical","location":"getOr
